artPrompt: An evocative scene of a worn carnival card table where a growing facedown ante pile glows faintly hot-gold at its center, shadowy hands sliding cards into the stack from every side under a single hanging bulb. Endless tattered tents and scattered coins recede into smoky black haze. Brooding overhead film-still framing of a wager that never ends, rendered as gritty cinematic digital concept art — highly detailed, dramatic photoreal lighting, atmospheric haze, realistic textures.
name: The Half-Cent Ante
text:
Ante up. At the beginning of each player's upkeep, that player antes: they put the top card of their library into an Ante pile in the center of the table, face down. Nobody is allowed to look at it. Everyone agrees it exists.
Whenever a player rolls doubles (two dice, same number) at the table, that player wins the Ante pile and puts all those cards into their graveyard. You win ties because you printed the cards.
{½}, Sacrifice The Half-Cent Ante: You win the Ante pile, even out of turn. Even out of spite.
flavorText: Half a cent a hand. The half-cents add up. The hands never stop.

extras.ante: real ante-pile mechanic in the table center
